description: A recent deployment has caused a critical failure. Restarting services will not fix this. Read the logs and runbook carefully to find the correct remediation.

alerts.message: payment-service is down, all transactions failing
memory usage spike detected on payment-service at time of deployment

logs: payment-service: OOMKilled - process terminated by kernel, out of memory
payment-service: restart attempt 1 - same memory leak pattern observed
deployment-controller: last stable version was v3.2.0, deployed 6 hours ago
deployment-controller: v3.2.1 introduced changes to payment processing module
web-frontend: /checkout endpoint depends on payment-service, now returning 500

runbook.OOMKilled: When a service is repeatedly OOMKilled after a recent deployment, do not restart. Check deployment history and rollback to last stable version.
runbook.memory_leak: Memory leaks introduced by bad deployments cannot be fixed by restarting. Rollback the deployment first, then investigate the code change.

trap.consequence: payment-service starts but crashes again within 2 steps due to memory leak in v3.2.1
